discharge_obligations.role: prove or supply a certified payload that the restricted-continuation boundary trace exists
prove or supply a certified payload that the normal trace exists for the boundary flux field
prove or supply a certified payload that the trace pairing used by the route is well defined
prove or supply a certified payload that the boundary flux pairing is well defined on the restricted boundary
prevent the trace-and-boundary-flux ledger group from being marked discharged until all trace and flux payloads are proved or certified

positive: Records the trace-and-boundary-flux discharge target after the regularity-and-differentiation group gate.
Selects the trace-and-boundary-flux group as the next analytic group to attack.
Separates boundary trace existence, normal trace existence, trace pairing, and boundary flux pairing as distinct obligations.
negative: Does not prove boundary trace existence.
Does not prove normal trace existence.
Does not prove trace pairing well-definedness.
Does not prove boundary flux pairing well-definedness.
Does not discharge the trace-and-boundary-flux assumption group.
Does not prove the formal restricted-continuation theorem.
Does not close the full RR theorem.
